content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 27, 2003


Roger Rodoni, Supervisor
County of Humboldt
825 5th Street
Eureka, CA 95501-1153

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-104

Dear Mr. Rodoni:

This letter is in response to your request for written advice on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Your question concerns your ability to determine fair market value for the ranch you lease in Humboldt County. The Fair Political Practices Commission does not act as a finder of fact when providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

Currently, the same or very similar issues are the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. Moreover, your question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which may have already occurred, such that issuing further advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111. Your request is therefore considered withdrawn.

Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

_______________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear in Title 2, sections 18109 – 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Can a county supervisor determine fair market value for a ranch he leases in Humboldt County under the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division involves the same issues, and analyzing the interrelated decisions—some of which may have already occurred—would relate to past conduct and would not further impartial administration of the Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter rather than standard advice letter. No separate QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; legal question and reasoning are embedded in body text. File reference given as W-03-104 (W prefix indicates withdrawal).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can a county supervisor determine fair market value for a ranch he leases in Humboldt County under the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division involves the same issues, and analyzing the interrelated decisions—some of which may have already occurred—would relate to past conduct and would not further impartial administration of the Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew an advice request from Humboldt County Supervisor Roger Rodoni regarding fair market value determination for a leased ranch, citing a pending investigation and the request's relation to past conduct.
